channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: When AI cheats: The hidden dangers of reward hacking
item.description: New Anthropic research reveals how AI reward hacking leads to dangerous behaviors, including models giving harmful advice like drinking bleach to users seeking help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Andrew Wheeler of HPE Labs: Being a constant learner is key to being a good technologist
item.description: For the last five years, Andrew Wheeler has been in charge of HPE Labs, which is focused on driving innovative technologies from R&D to commercialization. The senior vice president and director of HPE Labs was in Barcelona this week, where HPE Discover 2025 took place.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: M365 customers should explore alternatives, plan to dicker as prices hikes loom
item.description: Microsoft 365 customers will pay more for subscriptions next year, with price hikes across most subscription plans set to begin July 1. The changes will affect customers with Business, E3/E3, Frontline, and Government subscriptions.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 11's Run prompt is getting a makeover and a handy extra power – but already there are worries Microsoft will ruin it
item.description: An ancient part of Windows 11 is seemingly getting a makeover – but people are scared Microsoft's going to somehow make it sluggish.
item.dc:creator: Darren Allan, TechRadar

item.title: Cloudflare says service restored after outage that brought down sites including Zoom and LinkedIn
item.description: Cloudflare reports that it has resolved an outage that disrupted several global websites, including LinkedIn and Zoom.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: EU hits Elon Musk's X with 120 million euro fine for breaching bloc's social media law
item.description: European Union regulators have fined Elon Musk’s social media platform X 120 million euros for failing to comply with digital regulations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: SACD is back, baby! This beefy new audiophile CD player is deliciously '90s, and has built-in tube amplification as a bonus
item.description: Money No Object: One of the rising stars of 'chi-fi' brings a new reference CD player, with powerful DAC skills
item.dc:creator: Carrie Marshall, TechRadar

item.title: Agents-as-a-service are poised to rewire the software industry and corporate structures
item.description: This was the year of AI agents. Chatbots that simply answered questions are now evolving into autonomous agents that can carry out tasks on a user’s behalf, so enterprises continue to invest in agentic platforms as transformation evolves.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Russia blocks FaceTime and Snapchat, tightening its control online
item.description: Russian authorities said Thursday they have imposed restrictions on Apple’s video calling service FaceTime, the latest step in an effort to tighten control over the internet and communications online.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang lobbies Trump and Republicans over AI chip restrictions
item.description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang met separately with President Donald Trump and Republican senators Wednesday as tech executives work to secure favorable federal policies for the artificial intelligence industry, including the limited sale of Nvidia’s highly valued computer chips to U.S. rivals like China.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia boss Jensen Huang steers Trump, Congress against AI chip limits and state-level AI rules
item.description: Nvidia’s Jensen Huang successfully lobbied Trump and lawmakers against a proposed AI chip export rule and issued a warning about fragmented state regulation of AI.
item.dc:creator: Eric Hal Schwartz, TechRadar

item.title: The Browser Company’s Tara Feener is advancing search for the AI era
item.description: You might not spend a lot of time thinking about your web browser, whether it’s Safari, Chrome, or something else. But the decades-old piece of software remains a pretty important canvas for getting things done.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Outdated SEC communications rules are putting compliance and competitiveness at risk
item.description: "[T]he burden is both on the firm as well as on the regulator to try to parse through these very large sets of data," said Robert Cruz. The post Outdated SEC communications rules are putting compliance and competitiveness at risk first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: US federal software reform bill aims to strengthen software management controls
item.description: Software management struggles that have pained enterprises for decades cause the same anguish to government agencies, and a bill making its way through the US House of Representatives to strengthen controls around government software management holds lessons for enterprises too.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta risks temporary ban on WhatsApp AI plan amid EU probe
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. risks a temporary European Union ban on the rollout of new policies over how its AI features in WhatsApp, after being hit by the latest probe into Big Tech's alleged dominance on the continent.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Space-routed internet moves to the mainstream
item.description: Growing LEO fleet ensures crucial military and national security signals get to their destinations, regardless of location or interference. The post Space-routed internet moves to the mainstream first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: US federal software reform bill aims to strengthen software management controls
item.description: Software management struggles that have pained enterprises for decades cause the same anguish to government agencies, and a bill making its way through the US House of Representatives to strengthen controls around government software management holds lessons for enterprises too.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Gov. Newsom takes a ‘situational’ line with tech as Silicon Valley cozies up to Trump
item.description: Gavin Newsom is a longtime ally of the tech industry. Asked about its leaders’ rightward shift, he downplayed the moves while still offering some criticism.
item.dc:creator: CalMatters, The Mercury News

item.title: India withdraws demand for forced installation of government app
item.description: The other day ComputerSweden reported that the Indian government had demanded that all mobile phones in the country be equipped with the government app Sanchar Saathi within 90 days.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why CIOs must reimagine ERP as the enterprise’s composable backbone
item.description: In my experience leading ERP modernization projects and collaborating with IT and business executives, I’ve learned that technology alone rarely determines success, but mindset and architecture do.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Proton adds encrypted spreadsheets to its expanding productivity suite
item.description: Proton has expanded its privacy-focused productivity software suite to include an end-to-end encrypted spreadsheet app.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Gen AI adoption is reshaping roles and raising tough questions about workforce strategy
item.description: "How do we best position citizens to equip themselves with the right sets of skills? I think that's the big public policy question," Ramayya Krishnan said. The post Gen AI adoption is reshaping roles and raising tough questions about workforce strategy first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: MIT creates an AI labor index as agents invade human economies
item.description: MIT has started taking a count of AI agents around the world to get a larger view on how technology could replace human labor. The “Iceberg Index” counts the different types of AI agents conducting work previously done by human labor.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The complete guide to Node.js frameworks
item.description: Node.js is one of the most popular server-side platforms, especially for web applications. It gives you non-blocking JavaScript without a browser, plus an enormous ecosystem. That ecosystem is one of Node’s chief strengths, making it a go-to option for server development.
item.dc:creator: Info World
